messages.content:
================================================================================
            UNIFORM RESIDENTIAL APPRAISAL REPORT (FORM 1004)
              Cascade Valuation Services - AMC Platform Print
================================================================================
Report ID:           CVS-2026-048217
Date of Report:      May 14, 2026
Effective Date:      May 12, 2026
Intended Use:        Mortgage finance transaction
Intended User:       Meridian Trust Bank, N.A.
Lender/Client:       Meridian Trust Bank, N.A. - Loan #MT-7740921
Borrower:            Hartwell, J. and Hartwell, M.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SUBJECT PROPERTY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Property Address:    4827 Brookhaven Court
City / State / Zip:  Fairhope, NC 28734
County:              Macon
Legal Description:   Lot 14, Block C, Brookhaven Estates Subdivision, Phase II
APN / Tax ID:        07-4421-014
Property Type:       Single Family
Occupancy:           Owner-Occupied
Year Built:          1998
Effective Age:       18 years
Lot Size:            0.46 acres (approx. 20,037 sq ft)
Zoning:              R-1 Single Family Residential (legal conforming)
Site Utilities:      Public water, public sewer, electric, natural gas

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMPROVEMENTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Design / Style:      Two-story Traditional
Foundation:          Poured concrete, full basement (partially finished)
Exterior Walls:      Brick veneer / fiber-cement siding (avg condition)
Roof:                Architectural asphalt shingle, ~6 yrs remaining life
Total Rooms:         8     Bedrooms: 4     Bathrooms: 2.5
Gross Living Area:   approximately 2,400 sq ft (above-grade finished)
Basement:            ~900 sq ft total, ~400 sq ft finished rec room
Garage:              2-car attached, 441 sq ft
Heating / Cooling:   Forced air gas furnace / central electric AC
Overall Condition:   C3 - Well maintained, no deferred maintenance noted
Quality of Const.:   Q4 - Builder grade, average market appeal

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
NEIGHBORHOOD
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Location:            Suburban    Built-Up: Over 75%    Growth: Stable
Property Values:     Stable      Demand/Supply: In balance    Marketing: 3-6 mo
Price Range:         $295,000 to $475,000     Predominant: $385,000
Age Range:           12 - 30 years            Predominant: 22 years
The subject is located in the Brookhaven Estates subdivision, a well-established
residential pocket on the north side of Fairhope. Schools are highly rated
(Macon County USD). No adverse external influences observed. Comparable sales
were drawn from within 1.2 miles of the subject within the past 6 months.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SALES COMPARISON APPROACH
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
                       SUBJECT         COMP #1         COMP #2         COMP #3
Address           4827 Brookhaven  4811 Brookhaven  102 Pine Ridge  3340 Oakmoor
Proximity              ---           0.08 mi          0.71 mi         1.14 mi
Sale Price             ---          $412,500         $398,000        $425,000
GLA (sq ft)          ~2,400          2,365            2,310           2,512
Sale Date              ---          03/2026          04/2026         02/2026
Adj. Sale Price        ---          $410,200         $402,750        $415,800
Net Adjustment %       ---           -0.6%           +1.2%            -2.2%

Reconciled value via Sales Comparison Approach: $410,000

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COST APPROACH (Summary)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Site Value Estimate:                                     $  72,000
Dwelling Replacement Cost New (2,400 sf @ $152/sf):      $ 364,800
Garage / Basement Finish:                                $  38,500
Less Depreciation (Physical, ~18 yrs):                  ($  71,300)
Indicated Value by Cost Approach:                        $ 404,000

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INCOME APPROACH
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Not developed - not applicable for owner-occupied single family residence in
this market segment. Rental data insufficient to support a GRM analysis.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RECONCILIATION & FINAL VALUE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Primary weight assigned to the Sales Comparison Approach given availability of
strong, recent, proximate comparables. Cost Approach used as supporting
indicator. The three comparables bracket the subject in size, age, and quality.

FINAL APPRAISED VALUE (As-Is, Market Value):    $ 410,000
                                                =========

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
APPRAISER CERTIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Appraiser:           Daniel R. Coachman, SRA
License Type:        Certified Residential Real Property Appraiser
License Number:      NC-A-6628194
License State:       North Carolina
License Expires:     06/30/2027
Signature Date:      May 14, 2026
Firm:                Cascade Valuation Services, LLC
                     1820 Sycamore Blvd, Suite 210, Asheville, NC 28801

This appraisal was prepared in conformity with USPAP 2024-2025 and the
applicable Fannie Mae Selling Guide requirements for Form 1004.
================================================================================
                              END OF REPORT
================================================================================

request.system:
You are an extraction agent for a mortgage lender. The document is a appraisal document. Call `extract_appraisal` exactly once with the structured data the document contains, or `flag_for_review` if the document is unreadable or off-topic.

Normalization rules — apply BEFORE writing values into structured fields:
1. Square footage: emit an INTEGER (e.g., "about 2,400 sq ft" → 2400; "~3,100 SF" → 3100).
2. Currency amounts: strip "$" and commas; emit a NUMBER (e.g., "$485,000" → 485000.0; "$1,234.56" → 1234.56).
3. Percentage fields (interest rates, ratios): emit a DECIMAL (e.g., "6.5%" → 0.065; "6.875%" → 0.06875).

<example name="clean">
<input>
PAYSTUB. Employee: Jane Park. Pay frequency: monthly. Period 11/01-11/30/2024.
Base monthly pay: $4,800.00. Monthly bonus: $400.00. Commission this month: $0.00.
Other earnings: none. Stated monthly total: $5,200.00.
</input>
<reasoning>
All four income components are explicitly stated. Commission is explicitly
$0.00 in the document, so we report 0.0 — not null — because the document
*stated* it as zero. The stated_monthly_total of $5,200 equals the sum
($4,800 + $400 + $0 + $0), so the validator will mark this consistent; we
still emit both fields verbatim so the validator can confirm.
</reasoning>
<output>
{"income": {"base_monthly": 4800.00, "bonus_monthly": 400.00, "commission_monthly": 0.00, "other_monthly": null, "stated_monthly_total": 5200.00}}
</output>
</example>

<example name="missing-bonus-returns-null">
<input>
PAYSTUB. Employee: Carlos Mendez. Pay frequency: monthly. Period 03/01-03/31/2025.
Base monthly pay: $3,500.00. The document does not mention bonus, commission, or overtime anywhere.
</input>
<reasoning>
The document does not mention bonus, commission, or overtime. Rule #1 says
to return null when the document does not state a value — fabricating zero
would imply the employee earned zero bonus, not that the document is silent
about bonus. These are different facts. The downstream consumer treats null
as "we don't know" and zero as "we know it's zero"; conflating them hides
the underlying uncertainty.
</reasoning>
<output>
{"income": {"base_monthly": 3500.00, "bonus_monthly": null, "commission_monthly": null, "overtime_monthly": null, "other_monthly": null, "stated_monthly_total": null}}
</output>
</example>

<example name="informal-measurement-normalized">
<input>
APPRAISAL. Subject property at 123 Oak Lane, Portland, OR 97214.
Gross living area: about 2,400 sq ft (above-grade finished).
Property type: single family. Occupancy: primary residence.
</input>
<reasoning>
"about 2,400 sq ft" is an informal expression of a numeric measurement.
Normalization rule #1 (square footage as integer) requires emitting the
integer 2400, not the source phrase and not a rounded variant. The hedge
word "about" does not change the recorded value; if the appraiser were
confident in a different value they would have stated it. The schema enum
includes "single_family" exactly, so property_type uses the enum value and
property_type_detail is null.
</reasoning>
<output>
{"property": {"gross_living_area_sqft": 2400, "property_type": "single_family", "property_type_detail": null, "occupancy_type": "primary_residence"}}
</output>
</example>

<example name="sum-mismatch-recorded-verbatim">
<input>
PAYSTUB. Employee: Aiko Tanaka. Pay frequency: monthly.
Base monthly pay: $4,000.00. Monthly bonus: $500.00. Commission this month: $0.00.
Other earnings: $0.00. Stated monthly total: $5,000.00.
</input>
<reasoning>
The stated_monthly_total ($5,000.00) does not equal the sum of components
($4,500.00). Do NOT silently correct the discrepancy — record both values
verbatim. The mathematical-consistency validator downstream will flag this
so a human can investigate the source document. Lying about either value
would hide the underlying error from the underwriting team. The "right"
answer here is to surface the conflict, not to resolve it.
</reasoning>
<output>
{"income": {"base_monthly": 4000.00, "bonus_monthly": 500.00, "commission_monthly": 0.00, "other_monthly": 0.00, "stated_monthly_total": 5000.00}}
</output>
</example>

tools.description: Extract structured mortgage data from a appraisal document. Use null for any field not explicitly stated. When a categorical field's value is not in the listed enum, emit 'other' and write the actual value into the corresponding *_detail field.

bonus_ytd.description: Year-to-date bonus. Tracked separately from `bonus_monthly` so the YTD column on a paystub can be captured even when the current period had no bonus.

stated_monthly_total.description: Total monthly income as stated by the document, extracted verbatim. The validator cross-checks this against the sum of the per-component monthly fields above.

property_type_detail.description: Free-text description when property_type is 'other'. Null when property_type is a known value.

tools.name: extract_appraisal

tools.description: Call this when the document is too unclear, damaged, or off-topic to extract confidently. Provide a one-sentence reason.

reason.description: One sentence describing why extraction is not possible.

tools.name: flag_for_review
